completed_tasks.note: Root cause was /joint_commands feedback loop via control_gui._ext_cmd_callback firing FollowJointTrajectory action goals at ~60/sec. Fix: mirror publishes to /joint_commands_lerobot.

remaining_tasks.note: Most content baked into linux-env/CLAUDE.md; still needs user-facing setup doc.

blockers.description: Scene-side LEGO_USDS edits cannot use mid-session hot-reload
blockers.workaround: Always restart Isaac Sim cleanly (close + launch) when changing LEGO_USDS; never `touch extension.py` mid-session. Documented in isaac-sim-mcp/CLAUDE.md.

blockers.description: Duplicate USD references with same inner prim name → PhysX friction-patch explosion → quick_start hang
blockers.workaround: Spawn-skip approach (task 10) avoids this entirely by leaving LEGO_USDS unique. Per-instance USDs or post-reference inner-prim rename also work but are heavier.

human_actions_pending.action: Push commits to remotes (5 commits across 2 repos + 1 submodule)
human_actions_pending.context: All work is local. User said no pushes without approval. Branches: isaac-sim-mcp:so-arm101 (839b10d), Exploring-VLAs:main (61d10cb), lerobot:ros2-camera-on-main (9ef886ee).
From a misrouted bash test command. Untracked.

decisions.decision: Episode = 1 lego pick-place (not 2 per scene). Randomize after each episode.
decisions.rationale: User explicit direction. Simplifies loop, uniform episode lengths.

decisions.decision: Skip-spawn approach for cutting lego count (don't change LEGO_USDS shape)
decisions.rationale: Duplicate-USD refactor caused PhysX hang. Skip-spawn keeps the unique-USD invariant intact.
Avoids feedback loop through control_gui._ext_cmd_callback. Confirmed via GPT review.

decisions.decision: Drop_point uses grasp_home wrist_roll directly (no sign flip)
decisions.rationale: Real Test's Drop Scan has its own SCAN_WRIST_ROLL_RAD constant. Drop Point in QS doesn't need to flip — saves ~177° wrist swing per cycle.

decisions.decision: Module-level constants for new code in control_gui (not class-level)
decisions.rationale: Class-level constants don't survive _hot_reload_logic — instance's __class__ ref points to the OLD class.

decisions.decision: No real pick-place verification (Always PASS criterion)
decisions.rationale: Q1 from form: trust QS sentinel, no pose check. Verified by user.

decisions.decision: Re-randomize on QS halt, retry same lego (max 5 retries)
decisions.rationale: Q2 from form: maximizes success rate. May want to lower the cap in future.

uncommitted_files: Exploring-VLAs:vla_SO-ARM101/blue_2x3 (untracked stale test artifact, can rm)
next_action: Start with task 14 (episode = 1 lego) — it's the smallest behavior change and unblocks the new episode contract for everything downstream. Then task 10 (skip-spawn for fewer legos), then task 12 (black ground), then task 13 (timing audit), then task 11 (ArUco calibration), then tasks 15-17 (entry point + end-to-end verification).
context_notes: Recording works end-to-end. The phase 10.2 ORIGINAL blocker (handoff at start of session: 'pick-place doesn't run during recording') is resolved at the architecture level. Remaining work is scene polish (lego count, ground color, marker calibration), motion-quality tuning (durations), one episode-loop semantic change, and packaging the bring-up into a single entry point. The Record Sim tab is the production interface — driven by ros2 service rec_start/rec_stop from CLI, no GUI focus needed. CLAUDE.md docs across all 5 files are updated with this session's learnings, so a fresh Claude can come in with full context.
